conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to pick a caching strategy for our API. Options on the table are Redis, Memcached, and an in-process cache.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: In-process caching won't work because we run multiple server instances and the caches won't be in sync.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Memcached is fast but only supports simple key-value storage.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need to cache some sorted sets and pub/sub for cache invalidation signals, so Redis is clearly the better fit.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Actually one more thing — for the rate limiting specifically, I'll use a separate in-memory store per instance since it doesn't need to be globally consistent.

query: What did the user decide to use for general API caching?
